headline: The FBI reportedly won’t investigate ICE anymore
summary: Reports suggest the FBI will no longer investigate confrontations involving ICE agents. The DHS and DOJ have denied any policy change. This follows renewed scrutiny over ICE agent violence, including two reported deaths.

headline: Orchid is a delightfully retro and approachable hipster synth
summary: The Orchid synth offers a retro, approachable experience, reminiscent of classic chord organs. It allows users to play full chords with a single button, ideal for aspiring musicians. This review praises it as a delightful and easy-to-use piece of music gear.

headline: Birdfy's solar-powered smart feeder is down to one of its best prices
summary: Birdfy is hosting a midyear sale, with discounts up to 40% on smart bird feeders. The AI-Powered Smart Bird Feeder with Camera is now $149.99, marking its second-best price ever. This solar-powered device offers a convenient way to observe and track local birdlife.

headline: US Marshals arrest the Tate brothers in Miami
summary: Influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate were arrested in Miami by US Marshals. The arrest is linked to new rape and sex trafficking charges in England. British authorities are now seeking their extradition.

headline: The Odyssey Proves There’s Still Hope for Big-Budget Filmmaking
summary: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' is highlighted as a rare example of successful big-budget filmmaking. It showcases a singular artistic vision within a blockbuster format. The film creates a believable world, proving large studios can still produce quality cinema.

headline: Matt Damon Wants New Jason Bourne Movie to 'Be Like the First 3' as Work With Conclave Director Continues
summary: Matt Damon provided an update on the new Jason Bourne movie, expressing a desire for it to emulate the original trilogy. The project is moving forward with Edward Berger, known for 'Conclave' and 'All Quiet on the Western Front'. Damon's involvement suggests a commitment to the franchise's roots.

headline: The Best Deals Today: PNY RTX 5070 OC, Apple AirPods 4, The Dark Knight Trilogy, and More
summary: Today's top deals include discounts on tech and entertainment products. Shoppers can find offers on the PNY RTX 5070 OC graphics card and Apple AirPods 4. The Dark Knight Trilogy is also featured among the best current sales.

headline: Steam Machine Engineers at Valve Signal Grim Future as Memory Crisis Is 'Still Getting Worse'
summary: Valve's Steam Machine engineers warn that the tech industry's memory crisis is worsening. This ongoing issue leads to exorbitant prices for consumers. The future looks grim for hardware costs as the memory shortage persists.

headline: Burnham will be bolder but stick to Labour manifesto, deputy leader says
summary: Labour's deputy leader stated that Burnham will be bolder but remain committed to the party's manifesto. Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch criticized Burnham as a 'people pleaser' ahead of his expected prime ministership. This comes as Burnham prepares to become prime minister on Monday.

headline: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
summary: Russia executed a significant ballistic missile attack on Ukraine's two largest cities. The assault resulted in five fatalities and at least 35 injuries. This offensive occurs while Kyiv continues its own attacks on Russian infrastructure.

headline: Tate brothers arrested in US as further UK charges take total to 59
summary: The Tate brothers have been arrested in the US, facing additional charges in the UK. The Crown Prosecution Service has requested their extradition. Their lawyer maintains their innocence against the now 59 total charges.

headline: US and Iran trade fire after two US soldiers killed in Jordan
summary: The US and Iran have exchanged fire following the deaths of two US soldiers in Jordan. Kuwait reported an electricity and water plant fire, marking the second such attack in two days. Iran claims US strikes hit an under-construction nuclear power plant.
